woke up. The sun was in the window. Rex was already up. Rex wagged his tail. Pat got out of bed and put on his shoes. They went outside again. It was a new day.

Pat got the red ball. Pat threw the ball. Rex ran to it. Rex got the ball. Rex came back to Pat. Pat threw the ball again. Rex ran fast. Rex caught the ball. Pat said, "Good dog, Rex!"

The big tree was there. A bird was on the tree. The bird was small. The bird was blue. Pat sat down. Pat looked at the bird. The bird sang a song. The song was nice.

Mom called Pat. Mom said, "Come in." Pat said, "Bye, tree. Bye, bird." Pat walked to the door. Rex walked too. They went in.

In the house, Mom had soup. The soup was hot. Pat sat in the chair. Pat ate the soup. The soup was good. Pat ate it all.

Rex got a bone. Rex took the bone. Rex sat on the rug. Rex ate the bone. Rex was happy.

Pat sat with Mom. Mom read a book. The book had cats. The book had dogs. Pat looked at the cats. Pat looked at the dogs. The book was fun.

Mom said, "Time for a nap." Pat

was time for bed. Pat brushed his teeth. Pat put on pajamas. The pajamas had stars on them. Mom read him a book. The book was about the moon.

The moon was bright. The moon was round. The moon went across the sky every night.

Pat liked the book. Pat asked Mom to read it again. Mom read it again. Then Mom turned off the light.

"Good night, Pat," Mom said. "Good night, Mom," said Pat. "Good night, Rex," said Pat. Rex was on the rug next to the bed. Rex was already asleep.

Pat closed his eyes. The room was dark. The house was quiet. Outside, the wind moved the trees. The trees made a soft sound.

Soon Pat was asleep too. He dreamed about the park. He dreamed about Rex. He dreamed about Sam and the white dog. The dream was happy.

In the morning, Pat woke up. The sun was in the window. Rex was already up. Rex wagged his tail. Pat got out of bed and put on his shoes. They went outside again. It was a new day.

Pat got the red ball. Pat threw the ball. Rex ran to it. Rex got the ball. Rex came back to Pat. Pat

x wanted more. Mom said no. Rex sat down. Rex looked sad. Pat gave him a hug.

The day went on. Pat played with cars. The cars were small. The cars were red and blue. Pat made the cars go fast. Pat made the cars go slow. The cars went up the ramp. The cars went down the ramp. Rex watched the cars.

Then Pat read a book. The book had a cat. The cat had a hat. The cat sat on a mat. Pat liked the cat. Pat liked the hat. Pat liked the mat. Pat read the book two times.

Soon it was night. Pat had to brush his teeth. Pat had to put on his pj's. Pat had to get in bed. Mom read one more book. Then Pat closed his eyes. Rex got on the bed. Rex put his head on Pat's foot. They went to sleep.

In the morning, the sun came up. Pat got up. Rex got up too. Pat had toast. Pat had milk. Mom gave him a hug. Pat was ready for a new day. Pat said, "I love you, Mom." Mom said, "I love you, Pat." Rex wagged his tail. Rex was happy too.

Pat put on his coat. Pat put on his hat. Pat went out the door. The grass was wet. The sun was

it fast. The white dog wanted one too. Sam gave the white dog one. The white dog also ate it fast. Dogs eat fast.

A bird flew down. The bird saw the crackers. The bird wanted some. Pat broke a cracker. Pat threw a piece. The bird hopped over. The bird took the piece. The bird flew up to a tree. The bird ate the piece in the tree.

"That bird is smart," Sam said. "Yes," said Pat.

After a while, Sam said he had to go home. "It was nice to meet you," Sam said. "Yes," said Pat. "Nice to meet you too." They waved. Sam and the white dog walked away.

Pat and Rex stayed for a little longer. Then Pat said, "Time to go, Rex." Rex got up. Rex wagged his tail. They walked home together.

At home, Mom was in the kitchen. Mom was making soup. The soup smelled good. The soup was hot. Mom smiled when she saw Pat. "How was the park?" Mom asked. "Fun," said Pat. "I made a friend. His name is Sam."

"That is good," Mom said. "Wash your hands. Lunch is almost ready."

up was hot. Mom smiled when she saw Pat. "How was the park?" Mom asked. "Fun," said Pat. "I made a friend. His name is Sam."

"That is good," Mom said. "Wash your hands. Lunch is almost ready."

Pat washed his hands. The soap had bubbles. The water was warm. Pat dried his hands on a towel. The towel was soft.

Pat sat at the table. Mom put a bowl in front of him. The soup had carrots. The soup had peas. The soup had small pieces of chicken. Pat ate slowly. The soup was very hot.

Rex ate his food too. Rex had dog food in a bowl. The bowl was on the floor. Rex was a tidy dog. He did not spill.

After lunch, Pat colored a picture. The picture was of a dog. The picture was of Rex. Pat used brown for the body. Pat used black for the nose. Pat used pink for the tongue. The picture looked nice.

Mom hung the picture on the fridge. There were already three other pictures there. The new one made four.
